qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated admin RPC operations enable cluster compromise
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a race condition in session heartbeat handling in the dataserver (session.go, session_manager.go). This finding describes missing authentication on the coordinator admin RPC server (admin_server.go). Different vulnerable component (dataserver vs coordinator), different vulnerability class (race condition vs missing authentication), and different root cause (TOCTOU gap in KeepAlive vs no auth interceptor on admin endpoints).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-5gqc-qhrj-9xw8) describes a race condition between session heartbeat processing and session closure in oxiad/dataserver/controller/lead/session.go and session_manager.go, causing a server panic (send on closed channel). No qualifying finding identifies this root cause. The unauthenticated-admin-rpc finding concerns missing authentication on the coordinator admin RPC server (admin_server.go), which is a different component and different vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory root cause: race condition in heartbeat() blocking channel send while holding mutex in session.go, and TOCTOU gap in KeepAlive() in session_manager.go
unauthenticated-admin-rpc targets oxiad/coordinator/admin_server.go - different component (coordinator vs dataserver), different vuln class (missing auth vs race condition)
